summary: Full e2e testing of Gramin PAN Seva (FastAPI + MongoDB backend + single-file Bootstrap frontend). 36/36 backend pytest cases passed against public preview URL. Frontend e2e via Playwright verified admin & retailer logins, sidebar navigation (Super Distributors/Distributors/Retailers/Registration Approvals/PAN Status/Recharge Approvals/Rate Setup), live dashboard stats, wallet history, recharge request submission, support ticketing, notifications, and blocked-user rejection. No blocking bugs found.

minor.issue: CORSMiddleware uses allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' (split by comma). Browsers reject credentialed CORS with wildcard origin. App still works because frontend & backend share origin via /api proxy + Bearer token, but should set explicit origins for production.
No brute-force lockout/rate limit on failed logins; only successful logins are audit-logged. Failed login attempts are not recorded in audit_logs.
OTP record has no TTL/expiry — old codes remain valid until consumed; consider time-based expiry (e.g. 10 min).
No validation that amount > 0 or UTR not empty; negative/zero amounts would be accepted (and would credit 0 on approve).
Wallet debit and application insert are not atomic — if apply_wallet fails after insert, app stays Processed but no debit; vice versa unlikely. Consider transactional wrapping or compensating logic.

ui_bugs.issue: Submit button label is 'Submit Application' on the wallet-recharge page (retailer view); should read 'Submit Recharge Request' for clarity.

issues: No data-testid attributes anywhere in /app/frontend/public/index.html. Future automated tests rely on text/IDs/onclick handlers which is brittle. Add data-testid to login form, sidebar links, modal action buttons, table rows, and form fields.

action_items: (Optional, minor) Rename retailer recharge submit button text to 'Submit Recharge Request'.
(Optional) Add data-testid attributes across index.html for robust e2e automation.
(Optional) Tighten CORS: replace '*' + allow_credentials=True with explicit origins.
(Optional) Add OTP expiry timestamp check in /api/auth/register and rate-limit /auth/login.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend code is clean, async-correct, uses bcrypt + JWT, excludes _id from all responses, and routes are properly prefixed with /api.
Hierarchy/ancestors-based scoping is implemented correctly for users, PAN applications, recharge requests, tickets.
Pricing is read from settings on every PAN submit, so rate changes take effect immediately (verified by test).
FTP/SMTP are intentionally MOCKED — OTP returned in API response as dev_otp and uploads stored on local disk under /api/uploads. This matches the user-confirmed product choice.
Frontend index.html is ~3000+ lines single-file; consider splitting JS handlers into modules for maintainability but functional today.
register_invalid_otp test is slightly probabilistic (random OTP could match '000000'); acceptable for now.
test_credentials: AD-0001/Admin@123 (superadmin); SD1001/DT2031/RT10234/Password@123; Blocked RT10238 & Pending RT10236 (verified blocked).
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed users/registrations/PAN apps via API; no manual seed needed (seed_data.py auto-seeds on startup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend has NO data-testid attributes — use element IDs (#loginUserId, #loginPassword) and goTo('<view-id>') via page.evaluate for navigation. Sidebar view IDs: users-sd, users-dist, users-retailer, pan-status, wallet-recharge (both retailer recharge form and admin recharge approvals page), wallet-history, registrations, rate-setup, support, notifications. Bearer JWT stored in localStorage['gps_token']. OTP is MOCKED — POST /api/auth/register/send-otp returns dev_otp in response. Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py cover all 36 critical paths and pass cleanly against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
rca of the issue: No bugs requiring RCA — all features working as specified.
